design_summary.purpose: Small SRAM scratchpad controller demonstrating OpenRAM-style single-port SRAM integration, firmware-accessible MMIO registers, and MBIST collateral hooks.
design_summary.operating_model: A single synchronous clk domain accepts MMIO write/read strobes, controls a 64x32 single-port SRAM path, and latches BIST/IRQ state. The design keeps memory intent explicit for downstream synthesis and DFT flows by exposing the SRAM-facing intent signals and by retaining a fixed OpenRAM-compatible macro contract with a synthesizable fallback model.

module_architecture.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller
module_architecture.role: Top-level MMIO controller, SRAM scratchpad access front-end, and MBIST status/IRQ coordinator.
responsibilities: Decode the register map at addresses 0x00 through 0x24.
Latch and clear control/status/IRQ state according to the register contract.
Provide software-visible SRAM read/write access through MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_CONTROL, and MEM_RDATA.
Generate a single-port SRAM access abstraction for a 64x32 scratchpad.
Expose both external bist_start and register-driven BIST start semantics.
Track and report BIST done/fail/running and last_fail_addr[5:0].
Assert irq only when IRQ_ENABLE is set and a BIST done or fail condition is latched.
Preserve explicit memory intent for OpenRAM-compatible replacement or synthesis fallback.
interface_notes: Top-level MMIO ports are the firmware-facing control plane.
sram_clk/sram_csb/sram_web/sram_addr/sram_din are the explicit memory-intent outputs for downstream SRAM inference or macro binding.
sram_dout is the observed readback path from the selected SRAM implementation.
ready, bist_done, bist_fail, and irq are status outputs driven by the controller state machine and register image.
ownership_notes: The top module owns register file behavior and all software-visible status state.
The top module also owns the SRAM access orchestration and the BIST/IRQ latching policy.
Memory contents are not owned by the top module; they belong to the selected SRAM macro/model implementation.

module_architecture.name: demo_sram_32x64
module_architecture.role: OpenRAM-compatible single-port SRAM macro abstraction for a 64-word by 32-bit scratchpad.
responsibilities: Provide the canonical OpenRAM-compatible single-port SRAM interface.
Support synchronous read and write behavior suitable for single-port memory inference.
Expose a memory-like structure recognizable by downstream DFT and synthesis flows.
interface_notes: The interface uses clk, csb, web, addr[5:0], din[31:0], and dout[31:0].
csb is active-low chip select and web is active-low write enable.
The module name is reserved for OpenRAM-generated collateral when available.
ownership_notes: This module is the macro-facing memory contract and may be bound to physical SRAM collateral.
The controller drives its control/address/data signals and consumes its dout response.

module_architecture.name: demo_sram_32x64_model
module_architecture.role: Synthesizable fallback single-port memory wrapper used when OpenRAM macro collateral is not available during RTL generation.
responsibilities: Provide a synthesizable single-port SRAM surrogate.
Mirror the OpenRAM-compatible port list and control polarity.
Maintain memory intent for synthesis and DFT agents.
Serve as the fallback binding target when macro collateral is unavailable.
interface_notes: The interface matches the macro abstraction exactly: clk, csb, web, addr[5:0], din[31:0], dout[31:0].
No vendor-specific primitives are used in the fallback path.
The wrapper remains synchronous to clk and preserves the same memory intent as the macro abstraction.
ownership_notes: This module is the non-vendor fallback memory implementation.
It exists to preserve synthesis-safety and simulation continuity when macro collateral is absent.

interface_summary.intent: Drive the OpenRAM-compatible SRAM macro interface with explicit chip-select, write-enable, address, and data intent so downstream tools can recognize a single-port memory structure.
Drive the synthesizable fallback memory wrapper with the same control and data intent used for the macro path.
Return synchronous SRAM read data to the controller for MEM_RDATA capture and firmware-visible readback.
Return fallback-model read data to the controller when OpenRAM collateral is not present.

data_flow_summary.summary: wr_en, wr_addr, and wr_data update CONTROL, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, IRQ_CLEAR, and related state according to the fixed register map.
rd_en and rd_addr select rd_data content from CONTROL, STATUS, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_RDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, BIST_STATUS, IRQ_STATUS, and IRQ_CLEAR readback semantics as applicable.
MEM_ADDR and MEM_WDATA stage the target address and write data; MEM_CONTROL.MEM_WRITE requests a single-port write through the explicit SRAM interface using the 6-bit address and 32-bit data path.
MEM_ADDR and MEM_CONTROL.MEM_READ request a single-port read; the observed memory data is captured into MEM_RDATA and reflected back to firmware via rd_data when selected.

External bist_start and BIST_CONTROL.START are logically ORed into a single internal BIST request intent; BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT and IRQ_CLEAR clear latched status/IRQ indications as defined by the register contract.
When IRQ_ENABLE is set, latched BIST done/fail causes in IRQ_STATUS drive irq until cleared by IRQ_CLEAR or BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT.

clock_reset_summary.clocking: Single synchronous clock domain only. All controller and fallback memory behavior is synchronous to clk with a 50 MHz operating point and 20 ns period constraint. The SRAM abstraction is driven as an explicit single-port memory intent on the same clock.
clock_reset_summary.reset_behavior: reset_n is active-low and synchronous to clk. Reset clears control registers, status bits, BIST status, IRQ status, busy/running indicators, and software-visible readback data to zero. ready deasserts during reset and reasserts after reset release when the controller is idle. The SRAM interface is placed in an inactive, non-writing state on reset. The fallback SRAM model has no dedicated reset port and therefore receives only the clocked control intent.

integration_notes: The design intentionally preserves memory-like naming and a minimal SRAM port set to aid OpenRAM replacement, synthesis inference, and MBIST collateral generation.
If OpenRAM SRAM collateral is available, demo_sram_32x64 is the intended macro binding; otherwise demo_sram_32x64_model provides a synthesizable substitute with the same interface and naming intent.
BIST status registers remain present even if a downstream MBIST agent later replaces or wraps the memory path.
If memory and BIST are requested in the same cycle, BIST has priority over normal memory operations unless implementation collateral states otherwise.
IRQ_STATUS is latch-based and write-one-to-clear through IRQ_CLEAR, while BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT clears done/fail/IRQ status and last_fail_addr as specified.
The top-level SRAM ports are the authoritative memory-like interface for downstream detection and macro replacement.
consistency_notes: No hierarchy, ports, or filenames may differ from digital_spec_json.
